steps.title: Interpret zeros graphically
steps.content: Finding $f(x) = 0$ means locating where the curve intersects the x-axis. We count the x-intercepts.

steps.title: Count the x-intercepts from the graph
steps.content:
Reading the graph carefully, the cubic touches/crosses the x-axis at exactly two distinct x-values — at one location it crosses through, and at another it touches the axis (a double root from the squared factor) without changing sign.

So there are two distinct values of $x$ for which $f(x) = 0$.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
